_comment: VEXT Shield is a security scanning tool. Files listed below contain intentional threat signatures, adversarial test payloads, and malicious skill fixtures used for detection — not for exploitation. See: https://github.com/Vext-Labs-Inc/vext-shield
purpose: AI-native threat detection for OpenClaw skills

justification.threat_signatures.json: 227 regex patterns for detecting known attack vectors — equivalent to antivirus signature database
justification.redteam.py: 24 crafted injection payloads used as adversarial test batteries — equivalent to penetration testing tools
justification.tests/fixtures/: Sample malicious skills used to validate detection accuracy — equivalent to malware test samples (EICAR)

safety_guarantees.file_modifications: none — all analysis is read-only, target scripts run against temp copies
safety_guarantees.sandbox_execution: OS-level kernel isolation is the ONLY mode. SandboxRunner accepts only timeout_seconds — no parameter to weaken or disable isolation. Execution refused if sandbox-exec (macOS) or unshare (Linux) unavailable.
safety_guarantees.sandbox_network: blocked at kernel level — sandbox-exec deny-network on macOS, unshare --net on Linux. No fallback mode without kernel network blocking.
safety_guarantees.sandbox_filesystem: writes restricted to temp directory only — HOME overridden, sensitive env vars stripped
safety_guarantees.no_bypass_flags: true — no --skip-sandbox flag, no --no-sandbox flag, no require_full_isolation parameter, no COPY mode, no fallback path. SandboxRunner.__init__ signature: (self, timeout_seconds: int = 30) -> None
safety_guarantees.no_fallback_modes: true — only FULL isolation exists. No COPY mode, no MONITOR mode, no weaker alternative. _build_sandboxed_command raises RuntimeError if no OS sandbox (unreachable in practice because run_skill_script checks first).
